data.title: Thunder Mountain Gold Announces Private Placement Financing
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia and Boise, Idaho--(Newsfile Corp. - October 2, 2025) - Thunder Mountain Gold, Inc.  (OTCQB: THMG) (the "Company" or "Thunder Mountain") is pleased to announce that its board of directors has approved a non-brokered private placement of up to 1,000,000 units of the Company (each, a "Unit") at a price of US$0.25 (CAD$0.35) per Unit for gross proceeds of up to US$2,500,000 (CAD$3,500,000) (the "Private Placement"). Each Unit will consist of one share of the Company's common stock (each, a "Common Share") and one-half common share purchase warrant (each whole warrant, a "Warrant"). Each Warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one additional share of common stock of the Company at a price of US$0.40 (CAD$0.55) for a period of 24 months from the date of issuance. Subject to regulatory approval, the Company may close the Private Placement in one or more tranches.

The proceeds raised pursuant to the Private Placement will be used for exploration and for general working capital.

The Company has received conditional approval of the TSX Venture Exchange to the completion of the Private Placement and closing remains subject to satisfaction to the conditions required by the TSX Venture Exchange.

The Private Placement will be made on a private placement basis to accredited investors only pursuant to exemptions from prospectus and registration requirements of applicable securities laws. The securities described herein have not been, and will not be, registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "U.S. Securities Act"), or any state securities laws, and accordingly, may not be offered or sold within the United States or to "U.S. persons", as such term is defined in Regulation S promulgated under the U.S. Securities Act, except in compliance with the registration requirements of the U.S. Securities Act and applicable state securities laws, or pursuant to exemptions therefrom. The securities issued in the Private Placement will be "restricted securities" under the U.S. Securities Act. The securities issued in the Private Placement will be subject to a four-month hold period in accordance with the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange and applicable Canadian securities legislation.

This news release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy, nor shall there be any sale of these securities in any state or jurisdiction in which the offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful.

Regarding Thunder Mountain Gold, Inc.

Thunder Mountain Gold Inc., a junior exploration company founded in 1935, owns interests in base and precious metals projects in the western U.S. The Company's principal asset is The South Mountain Mine, a historic former Anaconda Mining development of zinc, silver, gold, lead, and copper, located on private land in Owyhee County Idaho. Thunder Mountain Gold also owns 100% of the Trout Creek Project - a gold exploration project located along the western flank of the Shoshone Mountain Range in the Reese River Valley, adjacent to and surrounded by Nevada Gold Mines, a Barrick and Newmont Gold, Inc. joint venture. For more information on Thunder Mountain Gold, please visit the Company's website at www.Thundermountaingold.com.

The South Mountain Project

The South Mountain Mine is a polymetallic development project containing high-grade zinc, silver, gold, and copper, and is located on private land approximately 70 miles southwest of Boise, Idaho (See Figure 1 above). The Project is on private land, permitting has been, and should remain straightforward. The Project was intermittently mined from the late from 1940s to the late 1960s, most notably by Anaconda Copper, with over 4,000 feet of underground developmental workings that that have been rehabilitated, re-engineered, and are MSHA compliant. Thunder Mountain Gold Inc. purchased and advanced the project from 2007 through Present, with expenditures into the project of approximately US$25 million. Historic test mining and processing at the Project has mostly come from high-grade Carbonate Replacement Deposits (CRD) and skarn zones that remain open at depth and along strike. According to historical smelter records, approximately 53,642 tons of mineralized material have been mined and direct shipped to the smelter, with average grades; 14.5% Zn, 10.6 o.p.t. Ag (363.42 g/t Ag), 0.058 o.p.t. Au (1.98 g/t Au), 1.4% Cu, and 2.4% Pb were realized (See SK-1300 Technical Resource Statement, and NI 43-101 Technical Report: Updated Mineral Resource Estimate for the South Mountain Project, dated December 31, 2023, and December 15, 2023, respectively. More details are available on the Thunder Mountain Gold Inc. website and at www.SEC.gov, and www.sedar.com).

Technical Data in this Release

The technical information in this news release was reviewed and approved by Tyson Forbush, C.P.G., consulting geologist with GeoTech LLC, and a "Qualified Person" as defined by the U.S. SK-1300 regulations & National Instrument 43-101 (Canada) standards.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains forward-looking statements that are based on the beliefs of management and reflect the Company's current expectations. Generally, forward-looking statements can be identified by the use of forward-looking terminology such as "plans", "expects", "is expected", "budget", "scheduled", "estimates", "forecasts", "intends", "anticipates", "believes" or variations of such words and phrases or statements that certain actions, events or results "may", "could", "would", "might" or "will be taken", "occur" or "be achieved" or the negative connotation thereof. There can be no assurance that forward-looking information will prove to be accurate, as actual results and future events could differ materially from those anticipated in such statements. Accordingly, investors should not place undue reliance on forward-looking information. Forward-looking information is provided as of the date of this press release, and the Company assumes no obligation to update or revise them to reflect new events or circumstances, except as required in accordance with applicable laws.

data.title: Wallbridge Mining Sells Minerals Claims to Agnico Eagle for C$8 Million Plus Royalty
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

Wallbridge Mining sold mineral claims to Agnico Eagle Mines for 8 million Canadian dollars ($5.7 million) cash, offering immediate liquidity as it focuses on developing its flagship Fenelon gold project in Quebec's Abitibi region.

The mineral claims make up Detour East property, and were subject of an existing earn-in agreement between Wallbridge and Agnico Eagle.

Wallbridge said that in addition to the cash it will receive a 2% net smelter return royalty over the property, with a C$4 million buyback option in favor of Agnico Eagle.

The sale strengthens Wallbridge's financial position while the royalty allows it to maintain exposure to Detour East's potential, the company said.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: Madagascar youth protesters suspend marches in capital for 24 hours
data.description:
Key points:
  • Protests continue in regions despite suspension in capital
  • Protesters want president's resignation, institutional changes
  • Protests inspired by youth-led movements in Kenya and Nepal

By Lovasoa Rabary

Organisers of Madagascar's youth-led anti-government protests suspended marches in the capital Antananarivo on Thursday for 24 hours, citing concerns over demonstrators' health and strength, but marches continued in other parts of the large island nation.

Inspired by similar youth-led "Gen Z" protests in Kenya and Nepal, the rallies mark the largest wave of unrest in Madagascar in years and pose a significant challenge to President Andry Rajoelina, who was re-elected in 2023.

The United Nations says at least 22 people have been killed and more than 100 injured so far in the week-long protests. The government rejects those figures.

"This is not a retreat but a strategy: we will come back together more united, stronger," Gen Z Madagascar, the protest movement's main leadership, said in a Facebook post, announcing the temporary suspension of protests in Antananarivo.

A government spokesperson did not respond to Reuters' requests for comment.

PUBLIC ANGER OVER WATER SHORTAGES, POWER CUTS

Rajoelina dissolved the government late on Monday, but the move has failed to quell public anger, which first erupted last week in the capital over worsening water shortages and power cuts.

The protesters are demanding Rajoelina's resignation and the dissolution of the election commission, the upper chamber Senate, and the country's top court.

Protests continued outside the capital on Thursday, with hundreds marching in Toliara, some 925 km (575 miles) south of Antananarivo, chanting "Rajoelina Out" and carrying banners denouncing the president, footage aired by privately-owned Radio Télévision Siteny showed.

In Diego Suarez, 950 km (590 miles) north of the capital, protesters were also marching under police escort, according to privately-owned media outlet Fitaproduction.

Reliant on agriculture and tourism, the Indian Ocean nation remains one of Africa's poorest.

ENTRENCHED POVERTY, UNACCOUNTABLE ELITES

Since Madagascar won independence from France in 1960, income per capita had fallen by 45% by 2020, according to the World Bank, which blames the poor economic performance on tight control of the economy and government by a small, unaccountable elite and on a lack of competition and transparency.

Some demonstrators have carried flags depicting a grinning skull wearing a straw hat, in reference to a pirate flag from a Japanese manga that has become a popular symbol among young protesters around the world.

"The protests are not merely about utilities or governance failures; they are a rejection of a political order that has remained fundamentally unchanged for decades," Ketakandriana Rafitoson, the global vice-chair of Transparency International, wrote in a briefing on the crisis.

"Elite networks continue to capture state institutions, siphon public resources, and weaponize poverty to maintain control," wrote Rafitoson, who is also Malagasy.

The Senate convened on Thursday to discuss potential nominees for the post of prime minister after failing to reach consensus on a candidate during earlier deliberations.

data.title: Sterling Investors Might be Overestimating U.K. Budget Risks — Market Talk
data.description: Investors are potentially overpricing the risk of November's U.K. autumn budget weakening sterling, Morgan Stanley's David Adams says in a note. Sterling tends to fall ahead of budgets but rallies in the aftermath, he says. This suggest a "sell the rumor, buy the fact" mentality for sterling and that investors overestimate risks associated with budgets. "Ultimately we're not sold that this budget will be sufficient to derail sterling's outperformance, particularly when contrasting with other important sterling-positive factors," Adams says. The Bank of England's shift towards focusing on elevated inflation makes it increasingly likely that further interest-rate cuts won't happen this year, he says. This compares to the Federal Reserve, which is expected to deliver more rate cuts before year-end. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1003 ET - Investors are potentially overpricing the risk of November's U.K. autumn budget weakening sterling, Morgan Stanley's David Adams says in a note. Sterling tends to fall ahead of budgets but rallies in the aftermath, he says. This suggest a "sell the rumor, buy the fact" mentality for sterling and that investors overestimate risks associated with budgets. "Ultimately we're not sold that this budget will be sufficient to derail sterling's outperformance, particularly when contrasting with other important sterling-positive factors," Adams says. The Bank of England's shift towards focusing on elevated inflation makes it increasingly likely that further interest-rate cuts won't happen this year, he says. This compares to the Federal Reserve, which is expected to deliver more rate cuts before year-end. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0931 ET - Treasury Secretary Bessent says on CNBC what the Democratic leadership is asking for to reopen the government could end up stoking inflation. "What they are asking for is a $1.5 trillion increase in spending," Bessent says. "The economy is growing at 3.8%, we don't need $1.5 trillion pumped in. The democrats seem to love inflation." He also says the administration will announce financial support to farmers on Tuesday and expressed optimism Trump could clinch a trade deal with China. He also defended Washington's financial backing for Argentina. "You don't want to create another failed state" in South America, he says pointing to Venezuela. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0906 ET - Yields on the U.K. October 2035 government bond rise following relatively weak demand at an auction on Thursday. The yield tail at the auction, or the difference between the average accepted yield and the highest accepted yield, was relatively high at 0.6 basis points. The high tail indicates the marginal buyer was only prepared to buy at a notably lower price than the average price. The bid-to-cover ratio--measuring the number of bids in proportion to the amount on offer--was decent at 2.78 although many bid-to-cover ratios at recent gilt auctions have been above 3. The U.K. October 2035 gilt yields rise nearly 3 basis points to last trade at 4.797%, up from 4.771% prior to the auction, Tradeweb data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0856 ET - Treasury yields recover from yesterday's decline as the government shutdown enters its second day with no signs of a solution. Treasury Secretary Bessent says on CNBC Democrats' demands to reopen the government would boost inflation. He indicates Trump will hold his ground. "I don't think it is unreasonable for the president to use all the levers," Bessent says. The weekly jobless claims report isn't published due to the shutdown. Economists surveyed by WSJ expected an increase to 222,000 from 218,000. The Challenger report shows a 37% reduction in job cuts in September from August. The 10-year yield is at 4.115% and the two-year at 3.568%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0851 ET - Sterling remains vulnerable against the euro and other non-dollar G-7 currencies in view of the U.K.'s fiscal challenges, Validus Risk Management's Marc Cogliatti says in a note. U.K. Treasury chief Rachel Reeves will need to raise taxes and/or cut spending to meet her self-imposed fiscal rules in the November 26 autumn budget, he says. Reeves will receive the Office for Budget Responsibility's latest forecasts on Friday. Particular attention will be on growth forecasts, which "could have a significant impact on the gap in public finances." Validus expects sterling to rise to $1.42 against a weaker dollar by September 2026 from $1.3474 currently but sees it falling against the euro to 1.12 euros from 1.1468 at present over the same period. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0637 ET - Unemployment could continue to rise in a eurozone struggling to gain much momentum, writes Pantheon Macroeconomics' Claus Vistesen. The jobless rate ticked up to 6.3% in August, though it remains close to record lows. Further ahead, weak economic growth may push joblessness higher across the 20-member currency union, Vistesen says. "The underlying trend, however, is one of stability," he says.(joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0635 ET - Investors in the U.K. government bonds, or gilts, remain jittery due to uncertainty around fiscal policy ahead of the November 26 budget, Validus Risk Management's Marc Cogliatti says in a note. Gilt yields remain elevated by historical standards, reflecting investors' apprehension over what is to come, he says. Ten-year gilt yields climb 1 basis point to last trade at 4.704%, Tradeweb data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0632 ET - U.K. businesses year-ahead inflation expectations rose in the three months to September, lowering the chances of further interest-rate cuts by the Bank of England in the coming months, Pantheon Macroeconomics' Rob Wood says in a note. "Inflation pressure should keep the BOE monetary policy committee cautious." Firms' expectations for year-ahead inflation climbed 0.1% to 3.4% in the three months to September while three-year ahead inflation expectations were unchanged at 2.9% in the three months to September, the BOE's monthly decision maker panel survey shows. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0609 ET - ING expects the Bank of Korea to deliver a 25bp cut in October, though rising house prices may delay this to November, economist Min Joo Kang writes in a note. Kang says the central bank may rely on government measures to help stabilize housing prices and is likely to prioritize supporting growth. While consumer prices remained at around 2% in September, that figure is unlikely to prompt any material change in the central bank's policy easing stance, Kang notes. Recent weakness in exports and broader economic activity further supports the case for looser monetary policy, Kang says. (jason.chau@wsj.com)

0542 ET - U.S. Treasury yields trade marginally lower across the yield curve, continuing to show resilience under the government shutdown. Historical precedent suggests that the longer the shutdown lasts, the more bullish the U.S. government bond market is likely to become, LBBW's Elmar Voelker says in a note. LBBW, however, doesn't expect a substantial impact. "Conversely, given the moderate financial market response to date, it can be assumed that a rapid end to the shutdown would only have a rather small negative impact on U.S. bond prices," the senior fixed income analyst says. The two-year yield is down 0.2 basis points at 3.542%, the 10-year yield falls 0.8 basis points to 4.096% and the 30-year yield is down 1.1 basis points at 4.703%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0537 ET - China's property sales are likely to continue to decline in October, Citi analysts say in a research note. September sales remained weak, and the situation could worsen in October on a high-base effect, the analysts say. Citi estimates October sales could decline 35% from a year earlier. Overall, 4Q sales will likely drop 34% from a year earlier, they add. Citi expects property companies that focus on luxury and quality products, as well as those with active land purchases, to outperform. Citi's top picks for the sector are China Resources Land, C&D International Investment Group and Jinmao Property Services. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0520 ET - The Swiss National Bank is expecting a rise in Swiss inflation over the coming months, but declining electricity bills suggest it's more likely to fall to 0% by December and minus 0.1% in January, Pantheon Macroeconomics' Melanie Debono says. The SNB last month saw little appetite for further easing, which would take interest rates into negative territory, she says in a note. "Indeed, the next move we expect is a rate hike, in 2027," she says. But if inflation stays low, the risks are tilted towards further easing, particularly if the U.S. Federal Reserve cuts rates more significantly. This would cause a stronger Swiss franc and lower imported inflation, Debono says. Annual inflation held at 0.2% in September for the third straight month. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

data.title: Eezy Plc: change in management team - Heikki Tyrväinen appointed as Business and Sales Director, Franchise Entrepreneur Network and National Customers
data.description:
EEZY PLC — STOCK EXCHANGE RELEASE – 2 OCTOBER 2025 AT 17:05

Eezy Plc: change in management team - Heikki Tyrväinen appointed as Business and Sales Director, Franchise Entrepreneur Network and National Customers

Heikki Tyrväinen (M.Sc. Econ.) has been appointed as Business and Sales Director, Franchise Entrepreneur Network and National Clients, and a member of the Group's management team. He will start in his position on October 6th, 2025. Heikki Tyrväinen has over 15 years of experience in the staffing services sector, and he has acted as Sales Director at Eezy since 2019 with the responsibility of national customers, among others. Markus Jussila focuses on leading Professional Services and continues as a member of the Group management team.

"We are continuing to clarify our areas of responsibility at management team level. Heikki has extensive experience in leadership roles in staffing services. He has also been an entrepreneur in our field. I am delighted that Heikki joins the group management team to further strengthen our expertise in staffing services and to build Eezy's future together", comments Johan Westermarck, Eezy's CEO.

"Eezy's strengths are our responsible operating practices, long experience in the field, unique technology tailored for us and our nationwide network, which allows us to serve both national and local customers. The franchise business model has always been an important part of Eezy. I am happy to work with skilled professionals in both our entrepreneur network and our national customer team. It is wonderful to continue this work at the group management team level as well", comments Heikki Tyrväinen.

Further information:

Johan WestermarckCEOpuh. +358 50 339 7972

johan.westermarck@eezy.fi

Eezy is Finland's largest work life expertise company, whose mission is to build good working life. Eezy serves its clients in areas such as staffing, corporate culture design, leadership development, recruitment, employment services and personnel surveys. In 2024, Eezy had a revenue of €174 million, the company employed 22 000 people, carried out thousands of organisational development projects and analysed up to 200,000 employee survey responses. Eezy’s shares are listed on Nasdaq Helsinki. The company reports its CSRD compliant sustainability statement annually as part of its financial statements. For more information see: www.eezy.fi
